title: Austrocylindropuntia exaltata
taxonomy: Austrocylindropuntia exaltata is a species of cactus that belongs to the family Cactaceae. It is a member of the genus Austrocylindropuntia, which is a group of cacti that are native to South America. The classification of Austrocylindropuntia exaltata can be broken down as follows: kingdom Plantae, clade Angiosperms, clade Eudicots, clade Asterids, order Caryophyllales, family Cactaceae, subfamily Opuntioideae, tribe Opuntieae, and genus Austrocylindropuntia. The specific epithet 'exaltata' refers to the plant's habit of growing in a shrub-like or tree-like form. This classification is based on the plant's morphological characteristics and its evolutionary relationships with other cacti.
